_meta.name: wardenclaw-doctrine
_meta.purpose: PUBLIC reference parameters for the WARDENCLAW doctrine strategy spec.
_meta.status: reference defaults — production deployments should calibrate on recent data (a calibration procedure is included in strategy-spec.md §11 and backtest/METHODOLOGY.md).
_meta.edge_protection_note: These are honest, backtestable defaults. They are NOT the live agent's calibrated values. The live trending-delta thresholds, tuned score bands, calibrated score->expected-move mapping, deployed ATR multiples, press/defend triggers and NET_EDGE_MIN_BPS as actually deployed live in a private runtime config and are intentionally never published here. Anything below that matches the live deployment is only the already-public conservative default in the repo's packages/core/src/config.ts; the tuned values are private.
_meta.units: bps = basis points (1bp = 0.01%). pct = percent. atr = multiples of recent ATR (ATR expressed as a fraction of price). usd = US dollars.

universe.benchmark: BNB (or an eligible-majors composite) — used as the relative-strength reference ONLY; the strategy never holds the benchmark as a position
universe.comment: Eligibility is keyed by exact token contract address, never by ticker symbol. The reference universe is the liquid-majors set plus the venue's eligible list.

_doc.redBenchmarkPct: benchmark 24h change (%) at/below which the trend signal votes RED
_doc.greenBenchmarkPct: benchmark 24h change (%) at/above which the trend signal votes GREEN
_doc.redFearGreed: CMC Fear & Greed at/below which the sentiment signal votes RED
_doc.greenFearGreed: CMC Fear & Greed at/above which the sentiment signal votes GREEN
_doc.redBreadth: fraction of tracked majors up at/below which breadth votes RED
_doc.greenBreadth: fraction of tracked majors up at/above which breadth votes GREEN
_doc.hysteresisChecks: consecutive confirming raw reads required to flip the committed regime
_doc.highVolatilityRatio: short-horizon move / majors baseline at/above which volatility is elevated

_doc.trendingDeltaMin: rank places of improvement required since the prior check (DELTA, not level)
_doc.trendingTopN: rank <= this counts as a fresh top-N entry
_doc.volumeExpansionMin: 24h volume must be >= this multiple of the token's own recent baseline
_doc.spikeCooldownChecks: consecutive post-peak consolidation checks required before a continuation entry
_doc.maxRetracePct: max fraction of the spike a pullback may retrace and still be a valid base
_doc.spikeMinPct: a run-up >= this fraction counts as a spike that arms the no-first-spike guard

_doc.rsOutperformMinBps: required outperformance vs the benchmark (24h change difference) in bps, on each confirming check
_doc.consecutiveChecks: number of consecutive checks that must clear the outperformance bar
_doc.requireRisingVolume: volume must be rising across the confirming checks

entry_momentum._doc: deterministic 0..100 score; component weights sum to 1. Momentum rotation between the strongest liquid major and stables, regime-gated (GREEN/NEUTRAL only).

_doc.netEdgeMinBps: REFERENCE margin the expected move must clear ABOVE modeled round-trip cost. Calibrate per family before deploying.
_doc.scoringSimCostBps: modeled simulated cost per leg (round trip = 2 legs) — the 'scored ledger' cost
_doc.frictionBudgetBps: round-trip friction budget; a setup whose size forces friction above this is not tradeable at this book size
_doc.walletFloorFraction: expected move must also exceed this fraction of the MEASURED real round-trip cost (wallet ledger sanity floor)
_doc.dustRoundTripCeilingBps: a measured real round-trip above this means notional is too small vs fixed cost (dust) — do not trade

_doc.kellyFraction: fractional-Kelly multiplier applied to the calibrated edge estimate
_doc.perTradeRiskPct: fraction of the book risked across the volatility stop band; size is DERIVED from the stop, never the reverse
_doc.maxPositionPct: hard cap on position size as a fraction of deployable capital
_doc.maxConcurrentPositions: concurrency cap (one position at a time)
_doc.competitionDqDrawdownPct: disqualifier drawdown cap (indicative 30% — confirm with the venue); a binding governor layer
_doc.internalWindowDrawdownPct: internal whole-window risk budget (binds before the DQ cap)
_doc.softDrawdownPct: soft warning level inside the daily limit
_doc.survivalLossStreak: consecutive realized losses that pause new directional entries

_doc.stopAtrMultiple: initial volatility stop distance below entry, in ATR multiples
_doc.breakevenTriggerAtr: unrealized gain (in ATR multiples) that arms the breakeven+costs ratchet
_doc.trailAtrMultiple: normal trail distance below the high-water mark, in ATR multiples
_doc.trailTightAtrMultiple: tight (DEFEND/RED) trail distance below the high-water mark, in ATR multiples
_doc.policy: stop only ever ratchets UP; no fixed take-profit (right tail stays open)

_doc.weeklyLegBudget: total swap legs budgeted for the window
_doc.flatBandLoPct: lower bound of the 'still flat' band that arms a PRESS trade late in the window
_doc.flatBandHiPct: upper bound of the 'still flat' band
_doc.defendTriggerPct: scored return above which the state switches to DEFEND (protect the lead)
_doc.pressMinScore: lowered minimum score for the single pre-committed PRESS trade
_doc.netEdgeDefendBonusBps: extra net-edge margin required while DEFENDing
_doc.pressStartDay: first competition day the PRESS trade becomes available
_doc.reservedLegsPerDay: legs reserved per remaining day so the budget is not spent early
_doc.comment: Generic competition/window-aware risk scheduling: full aggression early (HUNT), exactly one lowered-band trade if flat late (PRESS), tightened trail + raised thresholds when defending a gain (DEFEND).

_doc.microScoutUsd: notional for an optional stable<->stable last-resort trade that satisfies a minimum-activity rule without taking directional risk

expected_move_reference_prior._doc: REFERENCE PLACEHOLDER ONLY. expected_move_bps = max(0, (score - scoreFloor) * bpsPerScorePoint). This is a transparent naive prior so the spec is runnable out of the box; it is deliberately NOT a calibrated score->move mapping. Replace it with a calibration report built from real history (strategy-spec.md §11) before any real deployment. The live agent uses a private calibrated mapping that is not published here.
